footer_tagline: Free school lunch planner for Australian families.
about_subtitle: Making school lunches easier, healthier, and stress-free for Australian families.
about_intro: Welcome to Aussie Lunchbox Planner, your trusted partner in simplifying school mornings for Australian families.
about_mission1: We understand the daily challenge of packing lunchboxes that are nutritious, affordable, and actually eaten by kids. Our mission is to empower parents with automated, balanced meal plans that reduce decision fatigue and food waste.
about_mission2: Founded by a team of parents and tech enthusiasts in Sydney, we combine local supermarket data with kid-approved recipes to bring you a seamless planning experience.
about_feat1_desc: Generate a complete weekly plan in seconds. Say goodbye to the morning rush and hello to organized, stress-free starts to your day.
about_feat2_desc: We prioritize seasonal ingredients accessible in AU supermarkets to help you reduce food miles and minimize waste.
about_feat3_desc: Our algorithms ensure a mix of protein, fresh produce, and treats, aligned with Australian Dietary Guidelines for active kids.
about_community_desc: We are constantly improving based on your feedback. Together, let's make healthy eating the easy choice for our kids.
about_team_desc: We're not a faceless content farm. Aussie Lunchbox was started in Sydney by parents who were tired of googling the same lunchbox ideas every Sunday night. Everyone involved has school-age children in Australia.
about_editorial_desc: Everything we publish goes through a consistent review process. Here's exactly how we work.
about_important_title: Important: what Aussie Lunchbox is not
contact_subtitle: We'd love to hear from you! Whether you have a question, feedback, or a partnership inquiry.
contact_desc: If you have any questions about our meal plans, recipes, or how to use the planner, please drop us a message.

faq_subtitle: Everything you need to know about Aussie Lunchbox Planner.
faq_still_desc: Can't find the answer you're looking for? Send us a message — we'd love to help.
faq_q1: Is Aussie Lunchbox Planner free to use?
faq_a1: Yes! The core weekly planner — generating meal plans, viewing recipes, and downloading shopping lists — is completely free. We may introduce premium features in the future, but the essentials will always remain free.
faq_q2: How does the meal plan generator work?
faq_a2: Our planner selects a balanced mix of 5 lunch menus for the week from our curated database of AU-friendly recipes. It prioritises variety across categories (Sandwich, Hot, Baking, Cold) and applies any allergy or dietary filters you've set.
faq_q3: Can I filter for allergies or dietary requirements?
faq_a3: Yes. Before generating your plan, you can select from the following filters: Nut Allergy, Dairy Allergy, Gluten Allergy, Egg Allergy, Vegetarian, and Vegan. The planner will exclude any menus that contain those allergens.
faq_a4: We take allergy safety seriously and have carefully tagged every recipe in our database. However, we cannot guarantee that commercial products you buy are free from allergens — manufacturers change formulations and cross-contamination can occur. Always read the label on every product you purchase.
faq_q5: Where do the supermarket prices come from?
faq_a5: Price estimates are based on regular Woolworths AU prices, updated periodically. Prices are approximations and may differ at your local store or on sale days. Coles and ALDI prices may vary.
faq_a6: Calorie estimates are based on standard portion sizes and average nutritional values from generic food databases. They are rough estimates intended to give a ballpark idea of energy content.
faq_q7: Can I save or favourite meal plans?
faq_a7: Yes, if you create a free account (sign in with Google), you can save favourite meal plans to revisit later.
faq_q8: Can I download the shopping list?
faq_a8: Yes! Once your weekly plan is generated, you can download a PDF of the shopping list, grouped by category with estimated prices.
faq_q9: Is the app suitable for schools with nut-free policies?
faq_a9: Yes. Enable the 'Nut Allergy' filter and the planner will only suggest nut-free menus.
faq_q10: What languages does the app support?
faq_a10: Aussie Lunchbox Planner supports English, Korean (한국어), and Chinese (中文). Use the language selector in the top navigation bar to switch.
faq_q11: Can I suggest new recipes or report an error?
faq_a11: Absolutely! We'd love your feedback. Use our Contact page to send us recipe suggestions, report incorrect allergy tags, or flag any issues.
faq_q12: How do I use the fridge leftovers feature?
faq_a12: In the Planner sidebar, you'll find a 'Fridge Leftovers' field. Type in an ingredient you already have (e.g., 'chicken', 'spinach'), and the planner will prioritise menus that use that ingredient.
faq_q13: Does the app work on mobile?
faq_a13: Yes. Aussie Lunchbox Planner is fully responsive and works on smartphones and tablets.
faq_a14: We review and add new recipes regularly, typically monthly. Supermarket prices are refreshed weekly.
faq_q15: How do I create my first weekly lunchbox plan?
faq_a15: Getting started is simple and takes under a minute. Visit the Planner page and you'll see a sidebar with customisation options. You can set allergy filters (nut-free, dairy-free, gluten-free, vegan), exclude specific ingredients your child doesn't like, and even add fridge leftovers you want to use up. Once you're happy with your settings, click 'Generate Plan' and you'll have a complete 5-day school lunch plan within about 5 seconds. Each day includes a main lunch item with ingredients, estimated cost from Woolworths or Coles, and a suggested snack. You can then download the whole plan as a PDF or email the shopping list to yourself. No account is needed for the basic features — just open the page and start planning.
faq_q16: Can I customise the generated plan after it's created?
faq_a16: Currently, the planner generates a fresh 5-day plan each time you click Generate. If you don't like one of the suggested meals, the quickest approach is to regenerate — the planner picks from over 60 recipes, so you'll get a different combination each time. You can also influence results by adding ingredients you want to prioritise (using the 'Fridge Leftovers' field) or by excluding ingredients you'd rather avoid. We're working on a feature that will let you swap individual days without regenerating the whole plan, which we expect to launch later in 2026.
faq_q17: Does the planner support multiple allergies at the same time?
faq_a17: Yes, absolutely. You can enable as many allergy filters as you need simultaneously. For example, if your child is both nut-free and dairy-free, select both filters and the planner will only suggest meals that are free of both allergens. All six filters (nut, dairy, gluten, egg, vegetarian, vegan) can be combined in any combination. The planner will warn you if there are very few recipes available for your specific filter combination, so you'll know if you need to relax one of the restrictions to get a full 5-day plan.
faq_q18: How do you handle cross-contamination warnings?
faq_a18: Our recipes are tagged based on their listed ingredients — if a recipe doesn't contain nuts in its ingredient list, it's tagged as nut-free. However, we cannot account for cross-contamination that may occur during manufacturing of commercial products or in your own kitchen. For children with severe allergies (particularly anaphylaxis risk), we strongly recommend always checking the label of every product you purchase, even if you've bought it before, as manufacturers can change formulations and production lines. Our planner is a planning aid, not a medical device. For severe allergies, please consult your child's allergist or immunologist and inform the school directly.
faq_q19: Can I use the planner for a child with coeliac disease?
faq_a19: Yes, the gluten-free filter removes all recipes that contain wheat, barley, rye, and oats (unless specified as certified gluten-free). This covers the main gluten-containing grains. However, coeliac disease requires strict avoidance of even trace amounts of gluten, and cross-contamination in manufacturing is a real concern. We recommend using our gluten-free filter as a starting point for meal ideas, but always verify that the specific products and brands you buy are certified gluten-free and suitable for coeliac diets. Coeliac Australia (coeliac.org.au) maintains a list of certified products that can help with shopping.
faq_q20: How can I reduce my weekly lunchbox budget?
faq_a20: Several strategies can significantly reduce your lunchbox costs. First, use the planner's 'Fridge Leftovers' feature to incorporate ingredients you already have — this alone can save $5-10 per week. Second, compare prices between Woolworths and Coles for staples like bread, eggs, and pasta. Third, batch-cook on Sundays — making a large pot of pasta salad or a batch of muffins costs much less per serve than assembling individual lunches daily. Fourth, buy seasonal produce, which is cheaper and fresher. Our blog has a detailed budget guide with specific price comparisons between Woolworths and Coles for common lunchbox staples. Most families find they can prepare a week of nutritious lunches for $12-17 per child.
faq_q21: Do the prices include sales or specials?
faq_a21: No, our price estimates are based on regular shelf prices at Woolworths, updated periodically. We don't track weekly specials or loyalty card discounts because these change too frequently and vary by region. This means our estimates represent a 'worst case' scenario — you'll almost certainly spend less if you shop the specials. For the best savings, check the Woolworths and Coles apps before shopping to see what's on special that week, and adjust your plan accordingly.
faq_q22: Can I build a shopping list for two weeks at once?
faq_a22: The planner currently generates one week at a time. For a two-week shopping list, we recommend generating two separate plans and combining the shopping lists manually. Many parents find it useful to generate both weeks on Sunday, then buy the non-perishable items (canned goods, pasta, crackers, etc.) for both weeks at once, and pick up fresh items (bread, fruit, deli meats) at the start of each week. This approach reduces shopping trips while keeping ingredients fresh.
faq_q23: How do you ensure meals are nutritionally balanced?
faq_a23: Our recipe database is designed around the five food groups recommended by the Australian Dietary Guidelines (NHMRC): vegetables and legumes/beans, fruit, grain foods, lean meats and alternatives, and milk/yoghurt/cheese. Each weekly plan is built to include variety across these groups over the five days. While we don't calculate exact macronutrient ratios, we ensure that each day's lunch includes a protein source, a carbohydrate component, and at least one serving of vegetables or fruit. For specific dietary requirements, we always recommend consulting a registered dietitian.
faq_q24: Are portion sizes appropriate for different age groups?
faq_a24: Our recipes are designed for primary school children aged 5-12. Portion sizes are set for an 'average' child in this range — roughly 300-450 calories per lunch, which is appropriate for most school-age children. Younger children (Prep-Year 2) may not finish everything, while older children (Year 5-6) or very active kids might need a larger portion or an extra snack. We recommend adjusting quantities based on what your child actually eats — if they consistently bring food home, reduce the portion. If they're hungry after school, add an extra component like a piece of fruit or a muesli bar.
faq_q25: How do you calculate the nutritional information?
faq_a25: Our calorie and nutritional estimates are based on standard portion sizes using generic food composition databases, primarily the Australian Food Composition Database maintained by Food Standards Australia New Zealand (FSANZ). These are approximate values intended to give parents a general sense of energy content — they are not precise enough for clinical use. Actual nutritional values will vary depending on the specific brands and quantities you use.
faq_q26: Can I share my plan with my partner or co-parent?
faq_a26: Yes! The easiest way is to download the plan as a PDF and share it via email, messaging app, or AirDrop. The PDF includes the full 5-day menu, all recipes, and the complete shopping list. You can also email the plan directly from the planner using the 'Email Plan' button (requires a free account). We're considering adding shared accounts for families — let us know via the Contact page if this would be useful.
faq_q27: How do I report a bug or incorrect recipe?
faq_a27: We appreciate your help in keeping Aussie Lunchbox accurate! Head to our Contact page and select 'Technical Support' as the subject. Please include as much detail as possible: the recipe name, what seems incorrect (wrong ingredient, allergy tag error, broken pricing, etc.), and if it's a technical issue, your browser name and version plus a screenshot if possible. We review all reports and aim to fix confirmed issues within 48 hours for allergy-related errors, or within one week for other corrections.
faq_q28: Is my data secure? What do you store?
faq_a28: We take data security seriously. If you create an account, we store your email address, saved meal plans, and favourite recipes using Supabase (a trusted, SOC 2-compliant database platform). We do not store payment information, dietary health records, or any information about your children. Anonymous usage data (pages visited, features used) is collected via Google Analytics with IP anonymisation enabled. We never sell your personal data. For full details, see our Privacy Policy.
faq_q29: Can I use the planner offline?
faq_a29: The planner requires an internet connection to generate plans, as it calls our recipe database and pricing API in real-time. However, once you've generated a plan, you can download it as a PDF, which works fully offline. Many parents generate their plan on Sunday evening, download the PDF, and refer to it throughout the week — even without internet access.
faq_q30: How often is the recipe database updated?
faq_a30: We add new recipes to the database on a roughly monthly cycle, typically 3-5 new recipes per update. These go through our full review process: ingredient sourcing at Australian supermarkets, allergy verification, test preparation by a team member, and editorial review. Supermarket prices are refreshed more frequently — roughly every 1-2 weeks for core items. Seasonal adjustments happen at the start of each school term to ensure recipes reflect what's currently available and affordable at Woolworths and Coles.

home_testimonial1_text: I used to spend Sunday evenings stressing about the week's lunches. Now I generate a plan in seconds, download the shopping list, and I'm done. My kids actually eat everything because the meals are varied and kid-friendly.
home_testimonial2_text: My son has a severe nut allergy, and I was always anxious about what to pack. The allergy filters give me peace of mind — every meal is nut-free by default, and I can double-check ingredients easily.
home_testimonial3_text: We're on a tight budget and I was amazed at how much we save using the planner. The Woolworths price estimates are pretty accurate, and the batch cooking tips from the blog have been a game-changer.
home_commitment_desc: Every recipe and meal plan on Aussie Lunchbox is developed following Australian Dietary Guidelines (NHMRC). Our team of parent contributors tests each meal at home before publishing. We verify ingredient availability at Woolworths & Coles and update prices regularly.
about_advisory_desc: While we are not a medical service, our content approach is informed by established public health nutrition guidelines.
about_advisor1_desc: All nutrition content is benchmarked against the Australian Dietary Guidelines published by the National Health and Medical Research Council, ensuring our recommendations align with official dietary advice for children.
about_advisor2_desc: We reference Heart Foundation Australia's recommendations for children's heart-healthy eating patterns, particularly around reducing sodium and saturated fats in school lunches.
about_founding_story: It started in 2025 when three of us — all parents at the same Sydney primary school — realised we were spending Sunday evenings doing the exact same thing: searching for lunchbox ideas, checking prices at Woolworths, and trying to remember which kid had which allergy. We built Aussie Lunchbox to solve our own problem first. The tool worked so well for our families that we decided to open it up to everyone.
about_founding_story_continued: Since then, we've grown to a small team of parents, writers, and one very persistent spreadsheet enthusiast across Sydney, Melbourne, and Brisbane. Every recipe is tested in a real kitchen, every price is checked at a real supermarket, and every allergy tag is verified against real product labels.
